target: Annotate ISS-2218 closed-issues record with a closure_note acknowledging that the visible-rationale strategy (EXP-438) makes the Timestamp Error tab placement defensible but does not restore narrative coherence — closes ISS-2311 hole-6 audit-trail concern.

patches.value: Closed in the visibility sense by EXP-438 + post-integration wording fixes EXP-513 (commit 819cb05): future reviewers landing on the Overview reading guide or the Timestamp Error tab top will see a visible rationale for the standalone placement and read the placement as intentional rather than oversight. NOT closed in the narrative-coherence sense: the tab still inserts a methodology critique between two falsification arguments (Kill Shots → Timestamp Error → Predictions Analysis), and the visible rationale describes-but-does-not-rebut that ordering. The canary-visibility design goal (CLAUDE.md: "Timestamp Error tab, with cross-references from Overview, Section 1.4, and Part 6") forces this trade-off — restructuring would gain ~10 lines of internal arc continuity at the cost of cross-reference drift across ~10 inline references and the canary-prominence motivation that justifies the standalone placement in the first place. Future maintainers reviewing this closure should understand: placement is defensible (the canary constraint forces it; the rationale paragraphs make the choice visible), not coherent (a clean narrative-flow restructure was rejected, knowingly). HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole 4 (curmudgeon SEC-overview-timestamp-reading-guide.c1 hole 6 mislabels this as hole 3 — same underlying concern) is best understood as accepted-as-canary-cost rather than substantively resolved. Source: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/SEC-overview-timestamp-reading-guide.c1.json holes_found[5].
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon SEC-overview-timestamp-reading-guide.c1 hole 6 (minor) recommended: "Acknowledge the trade-off explicitly in the EXP-438 closure record. The HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole 3 is closed in the sense future reviewers will see the placement is intentional and read the rationale but NOT closed in the sense the narrative flow is now coherent. This is fine — the canary constraint forces the trade-off — but the closure ISS-2218 status note should reflect this distinction so a future maintainer reading the issue history does not assume the narrative-flow concern was substantively resolved." The closure_note is an additive field on the existing closed-issues record (does not alter status=fixed, fixed_by, fixed_at, integrated_exp), purely improves audit-trail honesty for future maintainers.

kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon is right that the visible-rationale strategy is partly circular as a closure. EXP-438 explicitly chose KEEP-AT-POSITION-8 over restructuring, and the patches it shipped describe-the-choice rather than rebut-the-narrative-flow-concern. The placement remains narratively orphaned by the standards HOL-NARRATIVE c2 itself applies (tabs 4-7 + 9-10 form a test-the-dome arc; tab 8 inserts a methodology critique mid-arc). The visible-rationale strategy makes that orphaning intentional and signposted, which is a real epistemic improvement over silent orphaning, but it is not the same thing as restoring narrative flow. EXP-438 partially acknowledges this in its anticipated_objections[2] ("A future curmudgeon review will still flag this... that is a curmudgeon prompt issue") — that response hand-waves the substantive point. The honest closure record should reflect that the operator chose canary-visibility over narrative coherence knowingly, not that the narrative concern was resolved.
why_it_doesnt_save_claim_for_other_audie: For the closed-issues audit-trail audience (future analysts, curmudgeon, tinker, operator-Claude sessions reading ISS-2218 to understand what was actually done), the kernel above is the load-bearing concern: the closure mechanism is correct (visibility rationale shipped to HEAD, verified by EXP-517), but the closure-reason framing as written ("expansion-integration") implies the narrative concern was substantively addressed. A future maintainer doing a HOL-NARRATIVE re-pass might re-flag tab orphaning, look up ISS-2218, see status=fixed and conclude the prior reviewer was wrong rather than understanding the prior reviewer accepted-as-canary-cost. The closure_note resolves that ambiguity without reopening the issue.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not just reopen ISS-2218 with status=accepted-trade-off rather than annotate? Annotating a closed issue obscures the fact that the narrative concern is not actually fixed.
anticipated_objections.response: Reopening would require deciding what action to take next — the canonical action is do-nothing-because-canary-constraint, which is what the closed-with-annotation state already encodes. Reopening introduces churn (decider re-triages, baby/Opus re-reads the same recommendation, curmudgeon re-reviews) for no actionable change. The annotation pattern is what closed-issues.json already uses for clarifying records without reverting status (cf. ISS-1933/1934/1935 closure_note pattern, ISS-2243 closure_reason_amendment pattern). The audit-trail honesty concern is fully addressed by the annotation; the status=fixed is correct in the sense that EXP-438's shipped patches landed and verified as intended.

anticipated_objections.objection: The annotation duplicates information already in EXP-438 anticipated_objections[2] and the SEC-overview-timestamp-reading-guide.c1 review. Why duplicate?
anticipated_objections.response: EXP-438 anticipated_objections[2] is buried inside an expansion file (read by curmudgeon-on-review once, then not by routine maintenance). The curmudgeon SEC-overview review is one of ~hundreds of review files, indexed only by point_id. A future maintainer querying ISS-2218 by ID (the natural lookup path: "what was hole 4 from HOL-NARRATIVE c2 and why is it closed?") would not naturally find either reference. The closure_note on ISS-2218 itself is the only field that surfaces in the obvious lookup path. This is the same rationale closed-issues.json already uses for closure_note in WIN-028.c8 verification cases — record the substantive fact at the lookup point.

anticipated_objections.objection: The annotation says the placement is "defensible, not coherent" — is that not the kind of editorial judgment that a future audit might want to revisit?
anticipated_objections.response: The judgment IS revisitable, and that is the point of writing it as a closure_note rather than a closure_reason_amendment. A closure_reason_amendment would imply the original closure reason was wrong; the closure_note is additive and clarifying. A future reviewer who disagrees with the defensible-vs-coherent framing can write a follow-up note or reopen with a new EXP — the audit trail will show the original 2026-05-30 closure, this 2026-05-31 annotation, and any future revision as three layered records. That is the correct shape for layered editorial reasoning.

anticipated_objections.objection: Hole numbering mismatch: curmudgeon SEC-overview-timestamp-reading-guide.c1 hole 6 says "HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole 3" but ISS-2218 title says "HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole 4" and the underlying HOL-NARRATIVE.c2 review confirms idx-3 (1-indexed: hole 4) is the timestamp tab orphan. Does this matter for the closure_note?
anticipated_objections.response: The hole-numbering mismatch in the curmudgeon writeup is a minor write-up bug (verified by reading HOLISTIC-HOL-NARRATIVE.c2.json holes_found[3] directly — it is the timestamp tab orphan at minor severity, matching ISS-2218 title "hole 4"). The closure_note above uses "hole 4" (the correct numbering per HOL-NARRATIVE.c2 itself) and adds a parenthetical noting the c1 review mislabels this as hole 3, to prevent a future maintainer cross-referencing both files from being confused by the numbering drift. No substantive impact on the annotation; just preempts a foot-gun.

anticipated_objections.objection: Should this be review_class: verification rather than deep-attack? The work is a single closure_note field addition, which is structurally a verification-class task.
anticipated_objections.response: The decider routed this as deep-attack and analyst-baby explicitly escalated it as "closure-record judgment beyond baby scope." The substantive judgment in the annotation (the "defensible, not coherent" framing, the canary-cost acceptance language, the layered-audit-trail rationale) is editorially load-bearing — a future curmudgeon-on-review pass should look at this annotation as a singleton, not batch it with other refinement work. Keeping deep-attack respects baby's escalation signal and matches the original tracker routing. The mechanical work is small (one field on one issue record); the framing judgment that goes into the text is not.

decider_action_recommended: Apply patch[0] to monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json: locate ISS-2218 and add the closure_note field with the value verbatim. Do not modify status, fixed_by, fixed_at, integrated_exp — additive only. Then close ISS-2311 with resolution "annotated ISS-2218 closure record per curmudgeon SEC-overview-timestamp-reading-guide.c1 hole 6 recommendation." The patch is one ISS lookup + one field add — no build run required (closed-issues.json is not rendered into docs/index.html).
summary_for_decider: ISS-2311 (minor, hole 6 from SEC-overview-timestamp-reading-guide.c1) recommends annotating the ISS-2218 closure record to acknowledge that EXP-438's visible-rationale strategy closes the audit-trail concern (future reviewers will see the placement is intentional) but does NOT restore narrative flow (tab 8 still inserts a methodology critique between two falsification tabs). Curmudgeon explicitly framed this as recordkeeping, not as a structural change — the canary constraint forces the trade-off. Proposed patch: add closure_note field to ISS-2218 with the layered framing "defensible (canary constraint forces visibility), not coherent (narrative flow remains broken, accepted-as-canary-cost)." Pattern matches existing closure_note usage on ISS-1933/1934/1935. Decider action: one-field add to closed-issues.json, no build needed, close ISS-2311 as resolved-by-EXP-488. Sibling closure note: this is the LAST of the SEC-overview-timestamp-reading-guide.c1 holes — 6 holes total mapped to 6 ISSs, holes 1/3/4/5 closed by EXP-513 commit 819cb05 (parallel-structure + we-voice + unilaterality + scope wording), hole 2 OBE-closed by EXP-517 (recipient-naming side-effect of EXP-513), hole 6 now closed by EXP-488. Review-class kept at deep-attack to honor baby escalation and editorial framing weight, even though mechanical work is verification-shaped.
